channel.title: Landing Page on Nicholas Lacourse
channel.description: Recent content in Landing Page on Nicholas Lacourse

item.title: About
item.description: My research studies behavioral economics with a focus on costly decision-making under uncertainty. I explore questions related to how monetary, cognitive, and physiological costs affect judgements about ourselves and others when the truth is not necessarily known. My work utilizes a variety of methods, but most utilize artefactual field experiments (online experiments) using representative sampling from either MTurk or Prolific. My work can broadly apply to fields such as industrial oraganization and job-search with a emphasis on information acquisition along with discrimination.

item.title: Research
item.description: Working Papers1 Sunk Costs in Performance Beliefs Art Is In the Eye of the Beholder: Judgements From Subjective Output Work in Progress Gender, Merit, and the Cost of Redistribution (with Oliver Hauser) Allocation Ambiguity (with Oliver Hauser) I Like Information, But Not From You: The Effects of Gender and Racial Salience on Performance Beliefs All papers including brief abstracts, manuscripts, pre-registrations, data, .do files, and appendices can be found in each papers dedicated Github repo.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: 2021 - 2022 I am currently not assisting or leading any modules for either undergraduate or graduate programs.1 2020 BEE2025: Microeconomics II-2 (Undergraduate) 2019 BEE1023: Introduction to Econometrics (Undergraduate) BEE2025: Microeconomics II-1 (Undergraduate) BEE2031: Econometrics (Undergraduate) 2018 BEE1022: Introductions to Statistics (Undergraduate) BEE1024: Mathematics for Economists (Undergraduate) This outcome is driven by COVID and my need to return back to the states for the remainder of my studies.

item.title: Press
item.description: 🛑 ⚠️ 🛠 This page is currently under construction. 🛠 ⚠️ 🛑

item.title: Contact
item.description: If you are a fellow academic and you are interested in collaboration on a project or want to reach out with a question regarding some of my work please contact me at my institutional addresses below: Email : nl362@exeter.ac.uk Office # : If you are from industry and are looking for consultation please email or call me at my personal addresses below: Email : nicholaslacourse.official@gmail.com Personal # : (936) 689-2207

item.title: CV
